Messages

  1. Faith entails commitment, and one must avoid a series of thoughts and actions. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا اجتنبوا...» (Faith is incompatible with suspicion, spying, and backbiting.)

  2. To avoid definite sins, we must avoid probable sins. (Therefore, since some conjectures are sin, we must avoid many conjectures.) «اجتنبوا كثیراً... انّ بعض الظن اثم»

  3. In a faithful society, the principle is trust, dignity, and the innocence of individuals. «انّ بعض الظن اثم»

  4. To prevent backbiting, the grounds for backbiting must be blocked. (The path to backbiting is first suspicion, and then investigation and spying; thus, the Quran prohibits them in that order.) «اجتنبوا كثیراً من الظن... لاتجسسوا ولا یغتب»

  5. Although sin appears sweet and pleasant outwardly, in the inner and celestial view, it is vile and repulsive. «یأكل لحم اخیه میتاً» (The inner reality of backbiting is eating dead flesh, and this type of expression is not used for any other sin.)

  6. Using examples and parables is a method of propagation and education. «أیحبّ احدكم...»

  7. In the method of forbidding evil, we should use emotional expressions. «لایغتب... أیحبّ احدكم ان یأكل لحم اخیه»

  8. Backbiting and speaking ill of others is forbidden, regardless of their age, race, or status. «لایغتب...» («احدكم» includes young and old, famous and unknown, scholar and ignorant, man and woman, etc.)

  9. Believing people are brothers and share the same blood. «لحم اخیه»

  10. Just as the dead person cannot defend himself, the person who is being backbitten cannot defend himself because he is absent. «میتاً»

  11. Backbiting is a type of predation. «یأكل لحم اخیه میتاً»

  12. Backbiting is incompatible with piety. «لایغتب... واتّقوا اللَّه»

  13. Piety leads man toward repentance. «واتّقوا اللَّه ان اللّه توّاب»

  14. There is no deadlock in Islam; past sins can be compensated for through repentance. «لایغتب... ان اللَّه توّاب رحیم»

  15. Allah's acceptance of excuses is accompanied by His Mercy. «توّاب رحیم»

  16. Allah's acceptance of repentance is a manifestation of His Mercy. «توّاب رحیم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰكُم مِّن ذَكَرٍ وَأُنثَىٰ وَجَعَلْنَـٰكُمْ شُعُوبًا وَقَبَآئِلَ لِتَعَارَفُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ أَتْقَىٰكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌ (۱۳)
O mankind! Indeed, We created you from a male and a female, and made you nations and tribes that you may identify yourselves with one another. Indeed the noblest of you in the sight of Allah is the most Godwary among you. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-aware. (13)

Key Points

Some commentators consider this verse related to the previous verses and have said: Since mocking and backbiting arise from a spirit of self-superiority and belittling others, this verse states: the criterion for superiority and honor is piety (taqwa), not gender or race. This verse refers to three important principles: the principle of equality in the creation of men and women, the principle of difference in human characteristics, and the principle that piety is the criterion for superiority. Of course, in other verses of the Quran, criteria such as knowledge, precedence, trustworthiness, capability, and migration are also seen.

Messages

1- Being male or female, or belonging to a certain tribe or nation, is not a criterion for pride, as these are the works of Allah. «خلقنا، جعلنا»

2- The differences seen in the form, appearance, and race of human beings are based on wisdom and are for the purpose of recognizing one another, not for boasting. «لتعارفوا»

3- Honor (karamah) in the sight of people is fleeting; attaining honor in the sight of Allah is important. «اكرمكم عنداللّه»

4- The Quran rejects all racial, partisan, ethnic, tribal, regional, economic, intellectual, cultural, social, and military discriminations, and considers piety (taqwa) to be the criterion of virtue. «ان اكرمكم عنداللّه أتقاكم»

5- Seeking superiority is inherent in human nature, and Islam has designated piety as the path for this innate desire. «انّ اكرمكم عنداللّه أتقاكم»

6- Let us not claim piety or pretend to possess it, for Allah knows everyone well. «علیم خبیر»

Ayah

۞ قَالَتِ ٱلْأَعْرَابُ ءَامَنَّا ۖ قُل لَّمْ تُؤْمِنُوا۟ وَلَـٰكِن قُولُوٓا۟ أَسْلَمْنَا وَلَمَّا يَدْخُلِ ٱلْإِيمَـٰنُ فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ ۖ وَإِن تُطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُۥ لَا يَلِتْكُم مِّنْ أَعْمَـٰلِكُمْ شَيْـًٔا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱٤)
The Bedouins say, ‘We have faith.’ Say, ‘You do not have faith yet; rather, say, ‘‘We have embraced Islam,’’ for faith has not yet entered into your hearts. Yet if you obey Allah and His Apostle, He will not stint anything of [the reward of] your works. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (14)

Key Points

The intent of al-A'rāb (the Bedouins) is the desert dwellers, some of whom were believers, as they are honored in Surah At-Tawbah: «و مِن الاَعراب مَن یُؤمن بِاللَّهِ و الیومِ الآخر»(128). But some of them considered themselves higher than they were and claimed faith (īmān), while they were nothing more than recent converts (Muslims).

The difference between Islam and Īmān

  1. Difference in Depth. Islam is an outward color (appearance), but īmān is adherence of the heart. Imam Sadiq صلى الله علیه وآله, regarding the Ayah «و مَن اَحسَنُ من اللَّه صِبغَة»(129), said: The Divine color is Islam. And in the exegesis of the Ayah «فقد استَمسَك بِالعُروَة الوثقى »(130), He said: Adherence to the firm Divine handle is īmān (faith).(131)

  2. Difference in Motivation. Sometimes the motivation for embracing Islam is to preserve or attain material benefits, but the motivation for īmān is certainly spiritual. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: By embracing Islam, a person's life (blood) is protected and marriage with Muslims becomes lawful, but the reward in the Hereafter is based on the faith of the heart.(132)

  3. Difference in Action. Declaring Islam is possible without action, but īmān must be accompanied by action, as we read in the tradition: «الایمانُ اِقرارٌ وعَمَل و الاسلام اِقرارٌ بلاعَمَل»(133). Therefore, Islam is inherent in īmān, but īmān is not inherent in Islam. In another tradition, Islam is likened to the Masjid al-Haram, and īmān is likened to the Ka'bah, which is situated in the middle of the Masjid al-Haram.(134) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Īmān is something that settles in the heart, and through it, a person reaches God, and action confirms that heartfelt belief. But Islam is something that appears in speech and behavior, even if it has not settled in the heart.(135)

  4. Difference in Rank. We read in a tradition: Īmān is higher than Islam, and piety (taqwā) is higher than īmān, and certainty (yaqīn) is higher than piety, and among people, nothing is rarer than certainty.(136)

Messages

1- We should not listen to every claim or slogan. «و قالتِ الاَعراب آمنّا»

2- We must restrain unfounded claims. «قل لم تؤمنوا»

3- Everyone should maintain their boundaries and not present themselves as more than they are. «قولوا اَسلَمنا»

4- Islam is an outward matter, but īmān relates to the heart. «فى قلوبكم»

5- One must speak to those who claim perfection in a way that does not make them despair of reaching perfection. «و لمّا یَدخُل الایمان فى قلوبكم»

6- The path to attaining īmān is practical obedience, not verbal claims. «لما یدخل الایمان... و ان تُطیعوا اللَّه و رسوله...»

7- Obedience to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is mentioned alongside obedience to Allah, and this is a sign of the Prophet's infallibility, requiring Him to be obeyed without question. «ان تُطیعوا اللَّه ورسوله»

8- Allah is Just and will not diminish a particle of the reward for a person's deeds. (Proper management is that not a particle of others' rights is neglected.) «لا یَلِتكم من اعمالكم شیئاً»

9- Obedience to Allah and the Messenger is the basis for receiving Divine forgiveness and mercy. «تطیعوا... انّ اللّه غفور رحیم»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَرْتَابُوا۟ وَجَـٰهَدُوا۟ بِأَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَأَنفُسِهِمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلصَّـٰدِقُونَ (۱٥)
The faithful are only those who have attained faith in Allah and His Apostle and then have never doubted, and who wage jihad with their possessions and their persons in the way of Allah. It is they who are the truthful. (15)

Key Points

The portrait of the true believer. Four verses of the Quran begin with the phrase "انّما المؤمنون" (Indeed, the believers are those who...), which depicts the portrait of the true believer: 1. «انّما المؤمنون الّذین اذا ذُكر اللَّه وَجِلتْ قلوبهم و اذا تُلیت علیهم آیاته زادتهم ایماناً و على ربّهم یَتوكّلون»(137) True believers are those whose hearts tremble whenever the name of Allah is mentioned, and when His verses are recited to them, their capacity for faith increases, and they rely solely upon Allah. 2. «انّما المؤمنون الّذین آمنوا باللّه و رسوله و اذا كانوا معه على امرٍ جامع لم یَذهَبوا حتّى یَستأذِنوه...»(138) True believers are those who believe in Allah and His Messenger, and when they are with the Prophet for a collective matter, they do not leave the scene without his permission. 3 and 4. They are mentioned in this very Surah; once in the tenth verse, «انّما المؤمنون اخوة...» (Indeed, the believers are brothers...), and again in this very verse.

If we place these four verses side by side, we discover the portrait of the sincere and true believers. They are the ones about whom the Quran has said: «اولئك هم المؤمنون حقّا»(139) (Those are the believers, truly) and «اولئك هم الصادقون» (Those are the truthful ones).

Therefore, true believers are those who: 1. Their hearts beat with the remembrance of Allah, not with wealth, status, or other things. «اذا ذكر اللَّه وَجِلَت قلوبهم» 2. They are constantly moving and evolving, and there is no stagnation in their work; in response to every divine message, they are committed, loving, and active. «و اذا تُلیت علیهم آیاته زادتهم ایمانا» 3. Their only reliance is faith in Allah, not treaties or dependencies on the East or West, etc. «على ربّهم یَتوكّلون» 4. In the social system, they follow the divine leader and do not act without his command, and they are loyal to him. «اذا كانوا معه على امرٍ جامع لم یَذهَبوا حتّى یَستأذنوه» 5. They do not consider themselves superior to others and look upon everyone with the eye of brotherhood. «انّما المؤمنون اخوة» 6. Their faith is stable. Their faith is based on knowledge, intellect, and innate disposition (fitrah), and by acting upon what they know, they have reached the degree of certainty. Propaganda and bitter or sweet events do not discourage them or cause them doubt. «ثمّ لم یَرتابوا» 7. Wherever necessary, they defend the ideology with their wealth and lives. «و جاهدوا باَموالهم و انفسهم»

(137) Al-Anfal, 2. (138) An-Nur, 62. (139) Al-Anfal, 4 and 74.

Messages

  1. The Quran both states the criterion and standard of perfection, «انّ اكرَمَكم عنداللّه اتقاكم» (Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you), and shows the perfect models. «انّما المؤمنون الّذین...»
  2. Belief in the Prophet is necessary alongside belief in Allah. «آمنوا باللّه و رسوله»
  3. The sign of true faith is stability and the absence of doubt in it. «ثمّ لم یَرتابوا»
  4. Faith is an internal matter that is recognized through action. «انّما المؤمنون الّذین... جاهدوا...»
  5. Faith without Jihad is nothing more than a slogan. «جاهدوا باموالهم و انفسهم...» (The perfection of faith lies in sacrificing life and wealth.)
  6. In Islamic culture, financial and physical Jihad must be in the way of Allah. «جاهدوا... فى سبیل اللّه»

Ayah

قُلْ أَتُعَلِّمُونَ ٱللَّهَ بِدِينِكُمْ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۱٦)
Say, ‘Will you inform Allah about your faith while Allah knows whatever there is in the heavens and whatever there is in the earth, and Allah has knowledge of all things?’ (16)

Key Points

A group swore by the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, saying that their faith was sincere. This Ayah was revealed [stating] that there is no need for an oath; Allah is aware of everything. Presenting one's beliefs to the Awliya' (friends/saints) of Allah is highly commendable if it is for evaluation, correction, or gaining assurance, just as Hadrat Abdul Azim al-Hasani presented his beliefs to Imam Hadi عليه السلام. However, if the presentation of beliefs is hypocritical, it deserves blame and reproach.

Messages

1- Displaying pretense before the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is, in reality, displaying pretense before Allah. Although this group showed pretense before the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, the Quran says: «أتُعلّمون اللّه...»

2- We should not make claims or display pretense before the God Who knows everything. «أتعلّمون... واللّه یعلم...»

3- Allah is aware both of the existence of things and of the characteristics of everything. «یعلم... وهوبكل شى علیم»

Ayah

يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيْكَ أَنْ أَسْلَمُوا۟ ۖ قُل لَّا تَمُنُّوا۟ عَلَىَّ إِسْلَـٰمَكُم ۖ بَلِ ٱللَّهُ يَمُنُّ عَلَيْكُمْ أَنْ هَدَىٰكُمْ لِلْإِيمَـٰنِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱۷)
They count it as a favour to you that they have embraced Islam. Say, ‘Do not count your embracing of Islam as a favour to me. No, it is Allah who has done you a favour in that He has guided you to faith, if you are truthful [in your claim]. (17)

Key Points

Some Muslims, such as the tribe of Banu Asad, considered their acceptance of Islam a favor (a "minnah") upon the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, saying: We embraced Islam willingly, and you should be grateful to us. This Ayah forbade them from this action. In this Ayah, Allah considers the blessing of faith (Iman) as His favor; in Ayah 164 of Surah Aal-i-Imran, He considers the sending of the prophets as His favor; and in Ayah 5 of Surah Al-Qasas, He considers making the oppressed (Mustad'afin) inheritors as His favor upon the people. This indicates that the most important blessings are the blessing of Divine guidance, infallible leadership, and the rule of truth (Haqq).

Messages

1- Being guided to Islam and accepting Islam is a great Divine blessing and a favor (minnah) from Allah. «بل اللَّه یمنّ»

2- Allah has no need or requirement for our Islam, faith, or worship. «لاتمنّوا علىّ»

3- The sign of sincere faith is considering oneself indebted to Allah, not placing a favor upon Him. «لاتمنّوا... بل اللَّه یمنّ... ان كنتم صادقین» (The true Muslim does not consider himself a creditor to Allah.)

4- Placing a favor upon the Prophet is, in reality, placing a favor upon Allah. (Allah responds to the favor placed upon His Prophet as if the favor was placed upon Him.) «یمنّون علیك... بل اللَّه یمنّ...»

5- You place a favor upon the Prophet for your acceptance of Islam, «یمنّون علیك ان اسلموا» while Allah is the One Who has guided you to the stage of faith (Iman). «هداكم للایمان»

6- The ultimate goal of human perfection is faith (Iman), «هداكم للایمان» not mere outward display of Islam, because hypocrites (Munafiqeen) also appear that way outwardly.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَعْلَمُ غَيْبَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بَصِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۸)
Indeed Allah knows the Unseen of the heavens and the earth, and Allah watches what you do.’ (18)

Messages

1- In the heavens and the earth, there are many hidden and undiscovered secrets. «انّ اللّه یعلم غیب السموات و الارض»

2- The hidden motives of humanity are sometimes far more complex than the unseen (Ghayb) of the heavens and the earth. (Regarding the heavens, the word «یعلم» [He knows] is used, and regarding the actions of humans, the word «بصیر» [All-Seeing/Perceiving] is used.)

3- Within the sphere of Monotheism (Tawhid), values are not based on outward show, obligation, or slogans, but rather on sincerity of the heart, knowledge of which belongs exclusively to Allah. «انّ اللَّه یعلم غیب السموات و الارض» (How could the God who knows the unseen and hidden aspects of existence not know our inner faith?)

5- Allah's knowledge is accompanied by His insight (Basirah). His knowledge is not general, superficial, one-sided, doubtful, or temporary. «ان اللَّه یعلم... واللَّه بصیر...»
